episode_title: Ole Reissmann: Revolutionizing a Legacy Newsroom with AI at Germany's Der Spiegel

theme_codings.theme_name: The Automation Paradox: Human-AI Labor Division in the Newsroom

best_quote.text: We run the fact checking tool not to get rid of the fact checking department, no no, to the contrary. So we run the fact checking tool which is in beta right now... And it only gets you like I don't know twenty, thirty, forty percent there. And the other percent, that's like the stuff humans are really needed for and which is really complicated and where you can't do a web search, like where people have to really dig into it.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann demonstrates the human-AI labor division through multiple concrete examples: AI-generated SEO headlines freeing editors for higher-value work, the fact-checking tool that gets 20-40% of the way, AI-powered text-to-speech for all articles, and the automated podcast prototype, while consistently framing AI as augmentation not replacement.

theme_codings.theme_name: Trust on the Line: Credibility, Transparency, and the Audience Contract

best_quote.text: We are really in the trust business and if we were to put something out, we want to make sure that not only a younger audience is happy, but our core audience, which tends to be a little bit older, our paying subscribers, we want to offer them factual information and we really want to make sure that we do not disappoint our readers and that we do not lose trust.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Trust preservation is a strategic priority driving Spiegel's cautious approach to consumer-facing AI products, with Reissmann explicitly choosing not to be first to market ('Better do this one right') and noting the generational divide between younger audiences comfortable with AI and older paying subscribers who expect factual accuracy.

theme_codings.theme_name: The Ethics Infrastructure: From Principles to Practice

best_quote.text: There's so much talk about, oh well, these AI LLMs, they are biased. And now it's actually an LLM that's helping us discover our biases. I think that's quite beautiful.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann describes principles-based rather than prescriptive approaches to AI policy, while the gender report project elegantly inverts the bias narrative by using AI to reveal Spiegel's own gender representation imbalance -- significantly more men than women appearing in coverage.

theme_codings.theme_name: Building the Machine: Technical Architecture and Engineering Choices

best_quote.text: I do not trust the LLM and the probability model... I'm gonna put some authoritative sources in there and ask about what did the experts actually say. And I think it gives you a much more interesting, nuanced answer compared to the LLM looks into itself and spills out some hallucinations and maybe some generalizations.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann describes Spiegel's most technically sophisticated AI infrastructure: a secure multi-model internal platform (Azure, ChatGPT, Google), vector database as foundational infrastructure for personalization, the fact-checking agent architecture with confidence scoring, the Chrome browser RAG plugin searching the Spiegel archive, and his personal use of Google NotebookLM as a RAG-based knowledge companion.

theme_codings.theme_name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling, Adoption, and the Knowledge Gap

best_quote.text: So far more than 870 people have taken part in these basic training sessions and when we announce them they are booked out, so we are offering more and more of them.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Spiegel has trained 870+ people in AI basics, with sessions consistently oversubscribed, complemented by a tool demonstration series and an upcoming company-wide AI immersion day, reflecting a deliberate strategy to democratize AI decision-making across the whole organization rather than concentrating it in leadership.

theme_codings.theme_name: Organizing for AI: Leadership, Strategy, and Institutional Change

best_quote.text: Maybe the most obvious use cases of generative AI are not the ones we should go after. Because like first we have to ask ourselves why we would want to use AI and what AI is actually good at. And does it serve our mission and does it align with our values?
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann embodies the 'why before how' approach as Spiegel's first Head of AI, managing approximately 10 concurrent projects with deliberate prioritization, choosing foundational infrastructure (vector database) over flashy consumer-facing products, and organizing three annual innovation cycles where every unit must evaluate AI opportunities.

theme_codings.theme_name: Power, Money, and the Platform: Big Tech and the News Industry

best_quote.text: In the past all the companies trained on our data. And we weren't like so aware or so acutely aware that this might be a problem. So I think there is a new sense of well, maybe let's protect our data.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann describes a new data protection consciousness, Spiegel's collaborative press archive with Axel Springer covering all German articles since the Federal Republic, and the necessity of inter-publisher collaboration because even major publishers are effectively small-to-mid-sized tech companies.

theme_codings.theme_name: Reimagining the News Product: Personalization, Formats, and Audience Experience

best_quote.text: I think we are at a new point where some of the things we thought were possible four years ago, maybe they are becoming more of a real reality in the next couple of months or years.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann envisions comprehensive product transformation: personalization built on the vector database, the multi-language automated podcast prototype, text-to-speech for every article, AI-powered summaries tested on Manager Magazin, the Chrome context plugin, and the potential 'second coming' of voice interfaces now that LLMs make them actually functional.

theme_codings.theme_name: Survival Economics: Business Models, Revenue, and the Market for AI Journalism

best_quote.text: If we were to deploy a user-facing product and potentially millions of people make use of these tools, that's for one costly. And then it's a question of, I mean, this compute, it doesn't come from nowhere, it costs energy, and is this a good use of energy right now.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann raises the economics of AI at scale, noting that while model costs are decreasing, user-facing products with millions of potential users remain expensive, and questions the sustainability/energy implications alongside the collaborative press archive as a shared-cost model.

theme_codings.theme_name: Regulating the Revolution: Law, Policy, and Governance of AI

best_quote.text: We can't control these software tools, right? So any other day there's a new function popping up and we want to have as many people as possible be capable of doing decisions on what to do and what not to do.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Regulation is touched upon indirectly through the challenge of software tools constantly adding AI features that may cross editorial lines, and through data protection requirements (European data residency via Azure).

theme_codings.theme_name: The Prototype Pipeline: From Experiment to Production

best_quote.text: This is a prototype, it's a proof of concept. Still, humans involved when the machine has generated the script, a native speaker has to look through the script and decides is this a good translation... I'm not sure this will see the light of the day, but we want to stay on top of the technological advancement and show what's possible.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Reissmann is candid about the prototype-to-production gap: the automated podcast is a proof of concept that may never ship, summaries are being tested on a sister publication before Spiegel itself, and the context tool is in internal testing before any wider rollout, reflecting disciplined proof-of-concept management.

theme_codings.theme_name: Data, Privacy, and the Boundaries of Responsible AI Use

best_quote.text: With Microsoft Azure they tell you you can choose to let the data stay in Europe which is for some people very important, for a company as Spiegel for sure.
best_quote.speaker: Ole Reissmann

theme_codings.summary: Spiegel built a secure internal AI platform with European data residency, reflecting data protection as a foundational design principle, while the gender report project demonstrates responsible use of AI for editorial self-auditing.
